name: Chablis Quiche
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 9-inch pie plate with pastry or use a frozen pie crust and rub some soft butter over the surface.
2. Partially bake the crust in a 425°F (220°C) oven for 8 minutes. Let cool until ready to use.
3. In a bowl, combine eggs, heavy cream, Chablis, nutmeg, salt, and pepper to taste. Beat until blended.
4. Gradually add grated Swiss cheese and beat well with the egg mixture.
5. Pour into the pie crust and bake in a 375°F (190°C) oven for 20 to 30 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
6. If the top is not golden brown, run under the broiler until browned. Allow to stand for 5 minutes before cutting.
7. Serve with a crisp green salad and chilled Chablis or Pinot Chardonnay. Serves 4 to 5.
Optional variations:
1. Line the bottom of the crust with overlapping pieces of thin ham (Prosciutto, Smithfield, Country, or boiled) and pour in the egg mixture, or dot with pieces of ham as you pour.
2. Add 4 to 5 slices of bacon cooked crisp and folded into the egg mixture.
3. Incorporate 1/2 cup shredded crabmeat or 1/2 cup finely diced shrimp into the egg mixture, or place on the bottom of the pastry shell.
4. Layer 1 cup thinly sliced onion (or 1 cup sliced leek, white part only) at the bottom of the crust.
5. Arrange 3/4 cup thinly sliced mushrooms over the bottom of the pastry shell or fold them into the egg mixture.
